questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client has fever, suspected infection, cool extremities, and a falling blood pressure despite increasing tachycardia.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client has fever, suspected infection, cool extremities, and a falling blood pressure despite increasing tachycardia.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability

center.text: Septic shock from suspected infection

leftActions.text: Obtain cultures and start broad-spectrum antibiotics
Administer isotonic crystalloid bolus as prescribed
Encourage oral fluids and reassess in 2 hours
Give acetaminophen as the primary intervention

rightMonitoring.text: Lactate trend and mean arterial pressure

questions.rationale: The pattern is septic shock with impaired perfusion. Early antibiotics, cultures, fluids, lactate, MAP, and urine output address the time-sensitive threats.

distractorRationales.sepsis-oral-fluids: Encourage oral fluids and reassess in 2 hours does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-antipyretic-only: Give acetaminophen as the primary intervention does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-daily-weight: Daily weight after stabilization does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-skin-turgor: Skin turgor alone does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the step-down unit.
questions.scenario: A 52-year-old client in the step-down unit has rapid deterioration after two hours of observation. The client has fever, suspected infection, cool extremities, and a falling blood pressure despite increasing tachycardia.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client has fever, suspected infection, cool extremities, and a falling blood pressure despite increasing tachycardia.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: rapid deterioration after two hours of observation

center.text: Septic shock from suspected infection

leftActions.text: Obtain cultures and start broad-spectrum antibiotics
Administer isotonic crystalloid bolus as prescribed
Encourage oral fluids and reassess in 2 hours
Give acetaminophen as the primary intervention

rightMonitoring.text: Lactate trend and mean arterial pressure

questions.rationale: The pattern is septic shock with impaired perfusion. Early antibiotics, cultures, fluids, lactate, MAP, and urine output address the time-sensitive threats.

distractorRationales.sepsis-oral-fluids: Encourage oral fluids and reassess in 2 hours does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-antipyretic-only: Give acetaminophen as the primary intervention does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-daily-weight: Daily weight after stabilization does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-skin-turgor: Skin turgor alone does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the urgent transfer report.
questions.scenario: A 38-year-old client in the urgent transfer report has high-risk findings that require immediate escalation. The client has fever, suspected infection, cool extremities, and a falling blood pressure despite increasing tachycardia.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client has fever, suspected infection, cool extremities, and a falling blood pressure despite increasing tachycardia.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: high-risk findings that require immediate escalation

leftActions.text: Obtain cultures and start broad-spectrum antibiotics
Administer isotonic crystalloid bolus as prescribed
Encourage oral fluids and reassess in 2 hours
Give acetaminophen as the primary intervention

rightMonitoring.text: Lactate trend and mean arterial pressure

distractorRationales.sepsis-oral-fluids: Encourage oral fluids and reassess in 2 hours does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-antipyretic-only: Give acetaminophen as the primary intervention does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-daily-weight: Daily weight after stabilization does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sepsis-skin-turgor: Skin turgor alone does not address the immediate sepsis / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client has polyuria, abdominal pain, Kussmaul respirations, dry mucous membranes, and a fruity breath odor.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client has polyuria, abdominal pain, Kussmaul respirations, dry mucous membranes, and a fruity breath odor.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability
DKA with dehydration and metabolic acidosis

center.text: DKA with dehydration and metabolic acidosis

leftActions.text: Start isotonic IV fluid replacement
Verify potassium plan before IV insulin infusion
Give sodium bicarbonate routinely for all DKA
Restrict fluids until glucose normalizes

rightMonitoring.text: Urine ketones once per shift only

questions.rationale: DKA care prioritizes volume restoration and safe insulin therapy. Potassium can fall quickly after insulin, so electrolytes and anion-gap closure must be trended.

distractorRationales.dka-bicarbonate-routine: Give sodium bicarbonate routinely for all DKA does not address the immediate endocrine / dka priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.dka-hold-fluids: Restrict fluids until glucose normalizes does not address the immediate endocrine / dka priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.dka-hba1c: Hemoglobin A1c only does not address the immediate endocrine / dka priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.dka-ketone-urine-only: Urine ketones once per shift only does not address the immediate endocrine / dka priority and could delay stabilization.

conceptNotes: DKA with dehydration and metabolic acidosis

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client reports crushing chest pressure radiating to the jaw with diaphoresis and nausea.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client reports crushing chest pressure radiating to the jaw with diaphoresis and nausea.

exhibits.title: Diagnostic Data
items: ECG shows ST elevation in contiguous leads

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability
Acute coronary syndrome with evolving infarction

center.text: Acute coronary syndrome with evolving infarction

leftActions.text: Administer chewable aspirin if not contraindicated
Activate reperfusion pathway and notify the provider
Ambulate to assess exertional tolerance
Delay ECG review until pain medication works

rightMonitoring.text: Chest pain, blood pressure, and perfusion

questions.rationale: STEMI care requires rapid antiplatelet therapy and reperfusion coordination. Rhythm, ST changes, pain, and perfusion show whether ischemia is progressing.

distractorRationales.mi-ambulate: Ambulate to assess exertional tolerance does not address the immediate cardiac / acs priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.mi-delay-ecg: Delay ECG review until pain medication works does not address the immediate cardiac / acs priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.mi-oral-intake: Oral intake percentage does not address the immediate cardiac / acs priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.mi-bowel-pattern: Bowel pattern does not address the immediate cardiac / acs priority and could delay stabilization.

conceptNotes: Acute coronary syndrome with evolving infarction

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client has sudden unilateral weakness, facial droop, slurred speech, and a known last-well time under 2 hours.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client has sudden unilateral weakness, facial droop, slurred speech, and a known last-well time under 2 hours.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability
Acute ischemic stroke within treatment window

center.text: Acute ischemic stroke within treatment window

leftActions.text: Activate stroke protocol and document last-known-well time
Prepare for noncontrast CT and thrombolytic screening
Give oral antihypertensive medication immediately
Offer food to assess swallowing

rightMonitoring.text: Focused neuro checks and NIHSS trend

questions.rationale: The priority is rapid stroke pathway activation without delaying imaging or eligibility screening. Neuro status and blood pressure guide treatment safety.

distractorRationales.stroke-oral-meds: Give oral antihypertensive medication immediately does not address the immediate neuro / stroke priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.stroke-food-swallow: Offer food to assess swallowing does not address the immediate neuro / stroke priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.stroke-daily-lipids: Daily lipid panel does not address the immediate neuro / stroke priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.stroke-routine-weight: Routine weight does not address the immediate neuro / stroke priority and could delay stabilization.

conceptNotes: Acute ischemic stroke within treatment window

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client is difficult to arouse with pinpoint pupils and slow shallow respirations after receiving opioid medication.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client is difficult to arouse with pinpoint pupils and slow shallow respirations after receiving opioid medication.

exhibits.title: Diagnostic Data
items: Medication record shows recent opioid dose

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability

center.text: Opioid-induced respiratory depression

leftActions.text: Support airway and ventilation with oxygen
Administer naloxone as prescribed
Allow the client to sleep and reassess later
Give another opioid dose for comfort

rightMonitoring.text: Sedation level and recurrent respiratory depression

questions.rationale: Ventilation comes first because opioid toxicity suppresses respiratory drive. Naloxone can wear off before the opioid, so recurrent sedation and oxygenation require close monitoring.

distractorRationales.opioid-overdose-let-sleep: Allow the client to sleep and reassess later does not address the immediate toxicology / respiratory depression priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.opioid-overdose-more-opioid: Give another opioid dose for comfort does not address the immediate toxicology / respiratory depression priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.opioid-overdose-appetite: Appetite after awakening does not address the immediate toxicology / respiratory depression priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.opioid-overdose-skin-temp: Skin temperature only does not address the immediate toxicology / respiratory depression priority and could delay stabilization.

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The postoperative client develops sudden dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, tachycardia, and anxiety.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The postoperative client develops sudden dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, tachycardia, and anxiety.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability

center.text: Pulmonary embolism with impaired oxygenation

leftActions.text: Apply oxygen and position with head of bed elevated
Notify provider or rapid response for anticoagulation workup
Massage the painful calf
Ambulate the client to relieve anxiety

rightMonitoring.text: Oxygen saturation and work of breathing
Blood pressure and heart rate trend

questions.rationale: PE can rapidly impair gas exchange and right-heart output. Oxygenation, hemodynamics, and urgent anticoagulation evaluation are higher priority than mobility or local leg care.

distractorRationales.pe-leg-massage: Massage the painful calf does not address the immediate respiratory / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.pe-walk-client: Ambulate the client to relieve anxiety does not address the immediate respiratory / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.pe-calf-size-daily: Daily calf circumference only does not address the immediate respiratory / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.pe-diet-tolerance: Diet tolerance does not address the immediate respiratory / perfusion priority and could delay stabilization.

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client reports severe limb and chest pain after dehydration with increasing fatigue.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client reports severe limb and chest pain after dehydration with increasing fatigue.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability

center.text: Vaso-occlusive sickle-cell crisis

leftActions.text: Administer prescribed opioid analgesia promptly
Start IV hydration and oxygen if hypoxemic
Apply ice packs to painful joints
Delay analgesia until imaging confirms crisis

questions.rationale: Vaso-occlusion requires rapid pain control, hydration, and oxygenation support. Cold exposure and delayed analgesia can worsen sickling and physiologic stress.

distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-ice-packs: Apply ice packs to painful joints does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-delay-pain-meds: Delay analgesia until imaging confirms crisis does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-calorie-count: Calorie count only does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-visual-acuity: Visual acuity only does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.

distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-calorie-count: Calorie count only does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-visual-acuity: Visual acuity only does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the urgent transfer report.
questions.scenario: A 38-year-old client in the urgent transfer report has high-risk findings that require immediate escalation. The client reports severe limb and chest pain after dehydration with increasing fatigue.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client reports severe limb and chest pain after dehydration with increasing fatigue.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: high-risk findings that require immediate escalation

center.text: Vaso-occlusive sickle-cell crisis

leftActions.text: Administer prescribed opioid analgesia promptly
Start IV hydration and oxygen if hypoxemic
Apply ice packs to painful joints
Delay analgesia until imaging confirms crisis

questions.rationale: Vaso-occlusion requires rapid pain control, hydration, and oxygenation support. Cold exposure and delayed analgesia can worsen sickling and physiologic stress.

distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-ice-packs: Apply ice packs to painful joints does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-delay-pain-meds: Delay analgesia until imaging confirms crisis does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-calorie-count: Calorie count only does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.sickle-crisis-visual-acuity: Visual acuity only does not address the immediate hematology / vaso-occlusion priority and could delay stabilization.

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The pregnant client has severe headache, visual changes, hyperreflexia, and severe-range blood pressure.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The pregnant client has severe headache, visual changes, hyperreflexia, and severe-range blood pressure.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability
Eclampsia or severe preeclampsia with seizure risk

center.text: Eclampsia or severe preeclampsia with seizure risk

leftActions.text: Administer magnesium sulfate as prescribed
Maintain seizure precautions and left-lateral positioning
Place in a dark room as the only intervention
Encourage walking to reduce anxiety

rightMonitoring.text: Respirations, deep tendon reflexes, and level of consciousness

questions.rationale: Magnesium reduces seizure risk, but toxicity can suppress respirations and reflexes. Blood pressure and urine output reflect maternal perfusion and renal involvement.

distractorRationales.eclampsia-dark-room-only: Place in a dark room as the only intervention does not address the immediate ob / hypertensive emergency priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.eclampsia-encourage-walking: Encourage walking to reduce anxiety does not address the immediate ob / hypertensive emergency priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.eclampsia-fetal-kicks-only: Kick counts only does not address the immediate ob / hypertensive emergency priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.eclampsia-appetite: Appetite and meal completion does not address the immediate ob / hypertensive emergency priority and could delay stabilization.

conceptNotes: Eclampsia or severe preeclampsia with seizure risk

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client develops throat tightness, wheezing, urticaria, and hypotension shortly after a new medication.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client develops throat tightness, wheezing, urticaria, and hypotension shortly after a new medication.

exhibits.title: Diagnostic Data
items: Medication administration record shows new antibiotic

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability
Anaphylaxis with airway and perfusion threat

center.text: Anaphylaxis with airway and perfusion threat

leftActions.text: Administer intramuscular epinephrine immediately
Support airway and give high-flow oxygen
Give oral antihistamine and wait for effect
Finish the medication infusion slowly

rightMonitoring.text: Airway swelling, breath sounds, and oxygenation

questions.rationale: Anaphylaxis is an immediate airway and circulatory emergency. Epinephrine and airway support are first-line while monitoring for recurrent bronchospasm or hypotension.

distractorRationales.anaphylaxis-oral-antihistamine: Give oral antihistamine and wait for effect does not address the immediate immune / airway priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.anaphylaxis-finish-infusion: Finish the medication infusion slowly does not address the immediate immune / airway priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.anaphylaxis-rash-only: Rash color only does not address the immediate immune / airway priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.anaphylaxis-temperature: Temperature every 4 hours does not address the immediate immune / airway priority and could delay stabilization.

conceptNotes: Anaphylaxis with airway and perfusion threat

questions.stem: Complete the bow-tie for the client in the emergency department.
questions.scenario: A 64-year-old client in the emergency department has new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability. The client receiving chemotherapy reports chills and malaise with a new fever.

exhibits.title: Nurses' Note
exhibits.body: The client receiving chemotherapy reports chills and malaise with a new fever.

exhibits.title: Focused Assessment
items: new confusion and worsening vital-sign instability

center.text: Neutropenic fever with sepsis risk

leftActions.text: Obtain cultures and start empiric IV antibiotics promptly
Initiate protective neutropenic precautions
Check rectal temperature for accuracy
Bring fresh flowers to improve mood

questions.rationale: Neutropenic fever can progress quickly to sepsis. Cultures, empiric antibiotics, protective precautions, temperature, ANC, and cultures target the immediate infection risk.

distractorRationales.neutropenic-fever-rectal-temp: Check rectal temperature for accuracy does not address the immediate oncology / infection priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.neutropenic-fever-fresh-flowers: Bring fresh flowers to improve mood does not address the immediate oncology / infection priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.neutropenic-fever-mouthwash-choice: Preferred mouthwash flavor does not address the immediate oncology / infection priority and could delay stabilization.
distractorRationales.neutropenic-fever-daily-weight-only: Daily weight only does not address the immediate oncology / infection priority and could delay stabilization.
